gxio_mpipe_equeue_t Struct Reference
[mPIPE Ingress and Egress Wrappers]

A convenient, thread-safe interface to an eDMA ring. More...


Data Fields

__gxio_dma_queue_t dma_queue
 State object for tracking head and tail pointers.
gxio_mpipe_edesc_t * edescs
 The ring entries.
unsigned long mask_num_entries
 The number of entries minus one.
unsigned long log2_num_entries
 The log2() of the number of entries.
gxio_mpipe_context_t * context
 The context.
gxio_mpipe_context_t * other_context
 The other mpipe context.
unsigned int ering
 The ering.
unsigned int channel
 The channel.
uint64_t rhwb_complete_count
 The remote buffer return count.
uint32_t rhwb_complete_bin [8]
 The remote buffer return counts, each one is for 1/8 segment of queue.
uint_reg_t inst_map
 Instance bit map.

Detailed Description

A convenient, thread-safe interface to an eDMA ring.

Examples:

mpipe/chaining/app.c, mpipe/forward/app.c, mpipe/forward/app_gx8072.c, and mpipe/loopback/app.c.

Field Documentation

__gxio_dma_queue_t gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::dma_queue

State object for tracking head and tail pointers.

gxio_mpipe_edesc_t* gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::edescs

The ring entries.

unsigned long gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::mask_num_entries

The number of entries minus one.

unsigned long gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::log2_num_entries

The log2() of the number of entries.

gxio_mpipe_context_t* gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::context

The context.

gxio_mpipe_context_t* gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::other_context

The other mpipe context.

Valid only with dual mpipe. Will be NULL with single mpipe instance.

unsigned int gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::ering

The ering.

unsigned int gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::channel

The channel.

uint64_t gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::rhwb_complete_count

The remote buffer return count.

uint32_t gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::rhwb_complete_bin[8]

The remote buffer return counts, each one is for 1/8 segment of queue.

uint_reg_t gxio_mpipe_equeue_t::inst_map

Instance bit map.

instance << MPIPE_EDMA_DESC_WORD1__INST_SHIFT Used to quick check if SW need perform remote buffer return.
